text.title: VUI Dialog Components and Prompt Class Rules
text.summary: Defines conversation components (intent, entity, prompt classes), dialog flow patterns, and rules for prompt copy.

section.title: Core Components
section.p: Every VUI turn consists of: wake word (activation), intent (user goal), entities (parameters), system prompt (question or confirmation), and response. Error handling is a first-class component, not an afterthought.

rule.statement: Design all four repair paths for every prompt node: no-match (unrecognized input), no-input (silence/timeout), max-retry (escalation after N failures), and escape-to-human (always available within 3 retries).
rule.rationale: VUI flows without repair paths produce dead ends. The user cannot read a "Page Not Found" equivalent — they just hear silence or repeated prompts and abandon the interaction.

rule.statement: Use the correct prompt class for each node: open prompts ("How can I help?") for unconstrained input; directed prompts ("What city?") when the entity space is large; option prompts ("Red, blue, or green?") only when options are 4 or fewer; confirmation prompts ("Is that correct?") for destructive or irreversible actions only.
rule.rationale: Option prompts with more than 4 choices exceed working memory. Using explicit confirmation for low-stakes actions (setting a timer) feels robotic and wastes turn budget. Implicit confirmation (just executing) is correct for low-stakes reversible actions.

rule.statement: Tag every prompt with class, intent, and retry-count in analytics. Without this tagging, you cannot measure where the dialog fails in production.
rule.rationale: Anonymous prompt firing data cannot be analyzed by failure type. Class + intent + retry-count gives the minimum dimensions needed to locate dialog bottlenecks.

example.note: Happy path, missing-entity slot-filling, and disambiguation flow for "set timer".
example.code:
Happy path: User: "Set a timer for 5 minutes" VUI: "5-minute timer started"
Missing entity (no duration): User: "Set a timer" VUI: "For how long?" User: "5 minutes" VUI: "5-minute timer started"
Disambiguation (ambiguous entity): User: "Set a timer for five" VUI: "5 minutes or 5 hours?" User: "Minutes" VUI: "5-minute timer started"
